title: MaskingRecipe
description:
A SEPAL Masking recipe. Apply the first band of `imageMask` as a per-pixel mask to `imageToMask` — pixels where the mask is zero (or masked) become masked in the output, pixels where the mask is non-zero pass through unchanged.

**What this recipe is for.** Combining a binary or fractional mask layer with an image of interest. Typical uses: mask non-forest from a continuous index, mask out water bodies, exclude cloud-contaminated pixels using an external cloud mask.

**Pipeline.** (1) Load `imageToMask` and `imageMask`. (2) Run `imageToMask.updateMask(imageMask.select(0))` — the FIRST band of the mask is what's actually used. (3) Output bands are inherited from `imageToMask`.

**Important: only the first band of `imageMask` is used.** The legacy schema had a `maskOptions` field with `band` / `values` / `negate` — these are NOT read by the EE backend and have been removed. If you need to mask by a specific band or by specific values, prepare the mask image upstream (e.g. via a Band Math recipe that produces a single-band binary mask).

**AOI semantics.** No separate AOI field — the AOI is the geometry of `imageToMask`.

**Fields the LLM must populate.** Both `imageToMask` and `imageMask` (each `{type, id}` — recipe ref or asset). The recipe has no defaults to lean on.

inputImage.title: MaskingInputImage
inputImage.description: A SEPAL recipe reference or Earth Engine asset id, with cached metadata. The GUI's `Form.AssetCombo` (or `RecipeInput`) populates `bands`/`visualizations` on selection — the LLM does not need to set those.

type.description: What `id` refers to. `RECIPE_REF` — id of another SEPAL recipe whose image output is consumed. `ASSET` — Earth Engine Image / ImageCollection asset id.

id.description: SEPAL recipe id (when type=RECIPE_REF) or Earth Engine asset id (when type=ASSET).

bands.description: Band names exposed by the source. Cosmetic — populated by the GUI on selection. The LLM should leave this absent.

visualizations.description: Optional visualization presets cached from the source. Cosmetic; the LLM should leave it absent.
